name: Mussel and Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 5 1/2 cups fresh corn (cut from about 3 ears)
1 pound black mussels, scrubbed well and beards pulled off
1 red bell pepper, cut into 1/4-inch dice
1/4 cup freshly chopped flat leaf parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring stock to a boil and add wine. Reduce heat to low and keep liquid warm.
2. In a 2-quart heavy saucepan, melt butter over moderately low heat and whisk in flour. Cook the roux, whisking, for 3 minutes.
3. Add 1 cup of the stock mixture and whisk vigorously until thick and smooth. Gradually add the remaining stock mixture, whisking continuously until it is a boil.
4. Reduce heat to maintain a simmer, allowing the mixture to reduce to 1 1/2 cups over about 5 minutes.
5. Stir in 3/4 cup of corn and continue simmering for an additional 4 minutes.
6. Pass the mixture through a food mill (avoid using a food processor) fitted with the medium disk into a bowl, then stir in cream and season with salt and pepper to taste. Keep the chowder warm.
7. In a large wok, heat oil over high heat until it begins to smoke. Add mussels and bell pepper, coating them well with oil for about 30 seconds. Cover and steam the mussels until they begin to open, around 3 minutes.
8. Stir in the remaining corn chowder, then simmer covered until more mussels open, approximately 2 minutes. Stir in the last 3/4 cup of corn and season with salt and pepper to taste.
9. Discard any unopened mussels as they will overcook. Serve the chowder in bowls, garnished with parsley. Accompany with bread.
Yield: 3 servings
